info.title: Tall Bob API
info.description: Tall Bob SMS, MMS, and messaging API for sending and managing communications, contacts, campaigns, and webhooks.

post.description:
To send a single chat SMS, make an HTTP POST to this resource URI and provide us with a to, message and reference for the chat and we will choose a longcode from your pool that the recipient can reply to.
Any additional requests received using the same to and reference will be sent using the same longcode.
Multiple recipients can use the same reference, but they will not necessarily receive the message from the same longcode.
Using multiple references to a single recipient allows the integrator to have multiple chat threads occurring on the end users device.
Required Parameters
When creating a new message via the API, you must include a to parameter. This value should be a single recipient phone number in E.164 format.
You must also include a message parameter containing the message's content. If this value exceeds a certain length it is regarded as a "multi-part" message.
Finally, you need to include a reference parameter, which you can use for your own internal tracking, as a transaction reference, when matching up against webhooks you have set up.
OPT-OUT tags
If your message contains the {OptOutURL} string, the length and price returned will reflect the expansion of this tag into an actual, proper URL - which could potentially turn your message into a multi-part message, with more than one segment.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

campaign_reference.description: (Optional Field) Used for custom report building

post.description:
Post data to create a new contact with details provided. Upon successful creation, details of your new contact will be returned - including the Tall Bob Contact ID, which is used by other methods, including sending an SMS Campaign.
Required Parameters
Every request must contain a first_name value, representing the contact's first name, and a value for their mobile number - in E.164 format. Neither parameter can contain an empty value.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

delete.description:
You can delete a contact using this endpoint. If your contact was also part of a group, it will automatically be removed from the group as well.
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

get.description:
Retrieve details about a specific contact, identified by Tall Bob Contact ID using this endpoint - your Contact ID must be present in the URL path.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

post.description:
If you do not know the Tall Bob Contact ID for your contact but you had set the "external ID" for the contact to your own unique ID, you can use that unique ID to find the contact, using this endpoint. In case your external ID contains special characters which may not translate correctly, if used as a query parameter, this is passed in the body of a POST.
Required Parameters
exernal_id - this is a unique ID which is shared between your system and ours, allowing you to match your contacts with Tall Bob contacts, using a common identifier.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

external_id.description: Your unique ID, which has been saved as the "external ID" of the contact (required)

get.description:
List of contacts for your account, by request. You can send a request and retrieve your contact list at a time that suits you, using this endpoint.
Optional filtering
You can also filter results by email address and mobile number, by passing these in as (optional) query parameters.
A Note about Postman Collections
If you have downloaded and imported this as a Postman Collection, you may find these optional query parameters have been selected with default values - which will probably not work.
If you are getting empty results back, check if these have been selected - and if they have, try again after deselecting them. You should then get back all your results, unfiltered.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

Get list of SMS Campaigns sent to a specific contact. You can send a request and retrieve data about SMS campaigns for a contact at a time that suits you, using this endpoint - your Tall Bob Contact ID must be present in the URL path.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

delete.description:
You can remove a specific metadata item from a contact, identified by Tall Bob Contact ID, using this endpoint - your Contact ID must be present in the URL path.
Required Parameters
Every request must contain a key parameter to identify which metadata value you wish to delete, by key name.
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

key.description: Your Tall Bob Contact metadata key name (required)

put.description:
You can create an unlimited number of additional metadata values against a contact, identified by Tall Bob Contact ID, using this endpoint - your Contact ID must be present in the URL path.
Required Parameters
Every request must contain a metadata parameter - a JSON object, containing any number of key/value pairs of contact metadata. If any metadata keys in your request already had values against them for your contact, they will be overwritten with the new values from your request.
Invalid Key Names
Certain characters cannot be used when defining key names for new contact metadata fields:



?
=
|
&
,
/





In addition, there are certain words considered "reserved" by the system, which can never be used to define contact metadata either - these are mostly used elsewhere, such as to identify a contact ID or their subscription status.

unsubscribed
FNAME
LNAME
URL
OptOutURL
KEY
VALUE
ID
firstname
lastname
mobile
email
externalID
aID
uID
uniqueID
createdFrom
CreatedDate_ts (with or without the underscore)
ModifiedDate_ts (with or without the underscore)
DeletedDate_ts (with or without the underscore)

Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

metadata.description: JSON object containing named key/value pairs of contact metadata (required)

put.description:
You can update a contact's details using this endpoint. Similar rules to the Create method apply, except there are no required parameters.
Opt-out status
You can also use this endpoint to "opt-out" (and also "opt-in") a contact by using the "unsubscribed" parameter in your request, as a boolean. (an integer 0 or 1 should also work).
NOTE!
If you pass in an unsubscribed parameter to your request, this is the ONLY part of the request which will be processed. You cannot combine updating a contact's details and subscription status both into the same request.
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

external_id.description: An "external" ID to match your own record for the contact - must be unique (max length: 100)

unsubscribed.description: Whether or not the contact has "opted-out" from further communication

post.description:
You can add a contact to your group, using this endpoint.
Required Parameters
Your request must contain both a contact_id parameter, representing your Tall Bob Contact ID and a group_id parameter, representing your Tall Bob Group ID.
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

Create a new contact group with details provided. Upon successful creation, details of your new group will be returned - including the Tall Bob Group ID, which is used by other methods, including sending an SMS Campaign.
Required Parameters
Your request must contain at least a name (representing the group's name) or external_id parameter. Both can be included. As with contacts, external IDs can be used as a common unique identifier between your system and ours.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

name.description: Name of group (required if external ID not present - truncated at 255 chars)

external_id.description: An "external" ID used to match against your own records - must be unique (required if name not present, max length: 100)

get.description:
Get list of groups for your account, by request. You can send a request and retrieve your contact group list at a time that suits you, using this endpoint.
Optional filtering
You can also filter results by group name, by passing in a name value as an (optional) query parameter.
A Note about Postman Collections
If you have downloaded and imported this as a Postman Collection, you may find optional query parameters have been selected with default values - which will probably not work.
If you are getting empty results back, check if these have been selected - and if they have, try again after deselecting them. You should then get back all your results, unfiltered.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

delete.description:
You can delete a group using this endpoint - your Tall Bob Group ID must be present in the URL path. If your group had any contacts added to it, they will automatically be removed from the group as well.
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

put.description:
You can update a group's details using this endpoint. Similar rules to the Create method apply.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

name.description: Name of group (required if external ID not present)

external_id.description: An "external" ID used to match against your own records - must be unique (required if name not present, max length: 100)

get.description:
List the contacts in a specific group. You can send a request and retrieve data about group members at a time that suits you, using this endpoint - your Tall Bob Group ID must be present in the URL path.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

delete.description:
You can remove a contact from your group, using this endpoint.
Required Parameters
Your request must contain both a contact_id parameter, representing your Tall Bob Contact ID and a group_id parameter, representing your Tall Bob Group ID.
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

get.description:
Retrieve status of a specific longcode using this endpoint - your longcode_address ID must be present in the URL path.
Possible Longcode Statuses:

pending: The longcode is under review or awaiting processing.

approved: The longcode has been reviewed and approved.

retired: The longcode is no longer active or has been decommissioned.


Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

post.description:
Post data to request a new longcode address with details provided. Upon successful creation, details of your new longcode request will be returned - including an ID, which is required by other methods to identify your newly created longcode request.
Required Parameters
Every request must contain a idn value, representing the Internation Dialing Number of the longcode requested , and a value for the comments field which represents the comments associated with a longcode request. Neither parameter can contain an empty value.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.



Name
Type
Description




idn
Number
International Dialing number for the country you are requesting a Long Code for


comments
String
Basic Description of use case for the Long Code


use_cases
ENUM Array
internal_comms, otp, transactional, marketing, notification, other

put.description:
You can retire an existing longcode address using this endpoint - your longcode ID must be present in the URL path.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

get.description:
Receive inbound MMS messages by request. You can send a request and retrieve inbound MMS messages at a time that suits you, using this endpoint. (note: MMS file/url details are unavailable from this endpoint, at this time)
Optional Filtering
You can filter your results by passing in optional query parameters to your request.



parameter
description
type




to
filter by specific longcode
integer


after
filter by setting lower limit on dates
timestamp


before
filter by setting upper limit on dates
timestamp



The format of timestamps in your request is reasonably flexible... For example, 01/01/1970, 2001-12-31, 2002-10-20T12:26:13+00:00, last Monday and yesterday are all examples of valid inputs for these types of request.
A Note about Postman Collections
If you have downloaded and imported this as a Postman Collection, you may find these optional query parameters have been selected with default values - which will probably not work.
If you are getting empty results back, check if these have been selected - and if they have, try again after deselecting them. You should then get back all your results, unfiltered.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

Receive MMS delivery reports by request. You can send a request and retrieve MMS delivery reports at a time that suits you, using this endpoint.
Optional Filtering
You can filter your results by passing in optional query parameters to your request.



parameter
description
type




to
filter by recipient mobile number
integer


status
filter by delivery status
string


after
filter by setting lower limit on dates
timestamp


before
filter by setting upper limit on dates
timestamp



The format of timestamps in your request is reasonably flexible... For example, 01/01/1970, 2001-12-31, 2002-10-20T12:26:13+00:00, last Monday and yesterday are all examples of valid inputs for these types of request.
A Note about Postman Collections
If you have downloaded and imported this as a Postman Collection, you may find these optional query parameters have been selected with default values - which will probably not work.
If you are getting empty results back, check if these have been selected - and if they have, try again after deselecting them. You should then get back all your results, unfiltered.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

post.summary: Send MMS To List of Mobiles
post.description:
If you want to send an MMS to a number of recipients, you can use this endpoint to send the same MMS to up to 1000 recipients in one go.
Required Parameters
You must include a to parameter. This value should be a list of recipient phone numbers in E.164 format.
You must also include a from parameter - this can be your phone number in E.164 format or an alphanumeric Sender ID - and a subject parameter, containing the subject line for the MMS. You can also include an optional message parameter, to accompany the file content.
The file content is provided using a url parameter. This must be a publicly accessible URL which requires no special auth headers to access the file and must be a link to a valid file type for MMS.
You can also include an optional reference parameter, which you can use for your own internal tracking as a transaction reference, when matching up against records you have set up in your own system, via webhooks you have set up in the platform.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.
Note: A single mobile number can also be used in the to field without needing to use an Array

to.description: Array of mobile numbers to send to - limited to 1000 (required)

from.description: Your Sender ID - can be a longcode (required)

subject.description: The MMS message subject text - limited to 60 chars (required)

url.description: URL for the file to be used in the MMS (required)

message.description: (Optional Field) message body text - limited to 5000 chars

shorten_urls.description: (Optional Field) When true, any well formed URLs in your message will be replaced with shortened links

campaign_reference.description: (Optional Field) Used for custom report building

post.description:
To send a single outgoing MMS message, make an HTTP POST to this resource URI.
Required Parameters
When creating a new MMS message via the API, you must include a to parameter. This value should be a single recipient phone number in E.164 format.
You must also include a from parameter - this can be your phone number in E.164 format or an alphanumeric Sender ID - and a subject parameter, containing the subject line for the MMS. You can also include an optional message parameter, to accompany the file content.
The file content is provided using a url parameter. This must be a publicly accessible URL which requires no special auth headers to access the file and must be a link to a valid file type for MMS.
You can also include an optional reference parameter, which you can use for your own internal tracking as a transaction reference, when matching up against records you have set up in your own system, via webhooks you have set up in the platform.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

from.description: Your Sender ID - can be a longcode (required)

url.description: URL containing the media to be included in the MMS message

campaign_reference.description: (Optional Field) Used for custom report building

post.description:
Post data to insert or update the consent to receive SMS messages
Scenarios where this endpoint may be of use to you are:

Where you have one or more locations wherein a customer can modify their consent for SMS messaging such as an account management system on your website
Where you may collect Opt out or consent withdrawal requests through a third party system or process, for example a support portal or a Call Centre
Where you are managing the Opt out process and providing customers with the ability to opt back in to receiving messaging through a third party platform.

To learn more about the different ways to manage your opt outs through Tall Bob check out our support article
Required Parameters
Every request must contain:

a mobile value, representing the Mobile number to be stored with the consent record
a type value containing either 'opt-in' or 'opt-out' to specify the type of consent record to be stored

Note: The mobile field must conform to the E.164 format in order for our system to properly process and store your request
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

type.description: Expected value must be opt-in or opt-out

post.description:
Receive an estimate of the price for sending a particular message.
The result returned contains an estimate of the cost of sending the message to a single recipient, as well as the number of message "segments" and whether or not any characters not in the standard GSM 03.38 character set were detected - we call these "unicode" messages.
IDN
Along with your message, you should include the IDN - or International Dialing Number - sometimes referred to as Country Calling Code in your request, in order to receive the most accurate price for sending to a particular country. Note the + sign is optional and the request will work with or without it.
Message Length
If the number of characters in your message exceeds a certain length it is considered a "multi-part" message. The results also include how many of these segments your message would comprise of.
Common Error Messages
Here is a list of common error message that you will find in your response while sending a messsage. All of these will result in a 400 response code

Recipient opted out

No valid recipients

Invalid sender ID

Recipient limit exceeded

Mobile is empty

Invalid mobile length

Invalid IDN


OPT-OUT tags
If your message contains an "opt-out" tag, the length and price returned will reflect the expansion of this tag into an actual, proper URL - which could potentially turn your message into a multi-part message, with more than one segment.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

idn.description: IDN you want to calculate price for (required)

Optional Filtering

A Note about Postman Collections
If you have downloaded and imported this as a Postman Collection, you may find these optional query parameters have been selected with default values - which will probably not work.
If you are getting empty results back, check if these have been selected - and if they have, try again after deselecting them. You should then get back all your results, unfiltered.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

Receive inbound SMS messages (replies) by request. You can send a request and retrieve message replies at a time that suits you, using this endpoint.
Optional Filtering
You can filter your results by passing in optional query parameters to your request.



parameter
description
type




to
filter by specific longcode
integer


after
filter by setting lower limit on dates
timestamp


before
filter by setting upper limit on dates
timestamp


campaign_id
filter by campaign id
string



The format of timestamps in your request is reasonably flexible... For example, 01/01/1970, 2001-12-31, 2002-10-20T12:26:13+00:00, last Monday and yesterday are all examples of valid inputs for these types of request. Campaign id may also be known as the sms id.
A Note about Postman Collections
If you have downloaded and imported this as a Postman Collection, you may find these optional query parameters have been selected with default values - which will probably not work.
If you are getting empty results back, check if these have been selected - and if they have, try again after deselecting them. You should then get back all your results, unfiltered.
Also please make sure that if you are using the 'before' and 'after' fields, that the dates should be URL encoded.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

post.description:
To send a single outgoing message, make an HTTP POST to this resource URI.
Opt-Out tags
If your message contains an "opt-out" tag {OptOutURL}, the length and price returned will reflect the expansion of this tag into an actual, proper URL - which could potentially turn your message into a multi-part message, with more than one segment.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.
Short URLs
If your message contains URL's and you would like it to be replaced with a shorter URL, or you would like to use our system to track engagement with the link, you can provide this endpoint with an optional boolean parameter shorten_urls, when this value is true any well formed URL (i.e. Must start with http:// or https:// ) will be replaced with a shorter one.
If your account has a pURL (Personalised URL) configured, it will use that configured domain for the URL.
By default the shortened URLs will look like http://vc4.co/c/ABCD where the "ABCD" will be replaced with a unique code for your URL. Note, these URLs are 20 characters in length, if your URL is longer than 20 characters, it will end up longer than it was originally. Any pURL configurations may make this length longer as well.
Required Parameters
When creating a new message via the API, you must include a to parameter. This value should be a single recipient phone number in E.164 format.
You must also include a from parameter. This can be your phone number in E.164 format or an alphanumeric Sender ID.
Finally, you need to include a message parameter containing the message's content. If this value exceeds a certain length it is regarded as a "multi-part" message.
You can also include an optional reference parameter, which you can use for your own internal tracking, as a transaction reference, when matching up against webhooks you have set up.

from.description: Your Sender ID - can be a longcode (required)

reference.description: (Optional Field) Transaction reference for tracking messages

shorten_urls.description: (Optional Field) When true, any well formed URLs in your message will be replaced with shortened links

campaign_reference.description: (Optional Field) Used for custom report building

post.summary: Send SMS To List of Mobiles
post.description:
Basic usage
If you want to send to a number of recipients, you can use this endpoint to send the same message to up to 1000 recipients in one go.
Personalised usage
If you want to send 'personalised' messages (a slightly different message to each recipient) to your recipients, you can also use this end point.
Personalisation is done using the liquid template engine. To personalise a message the use of double curly braces should be used to enable the replacement.. e.g. {{ name }}
Default values can also be provided and will be used if no data was supplied. e.g. {{ name | default: 'Bob' }}
Note: If no data and no default value is provided, the content inside the double curly braces will be replaced by an empty string.
Note: Default values can use pairs of ' or " Because the API uses JSON, caution should be used during development around the double quote, " These characters need to appear escaped within the message. e.g. default: \"Tall\"
Opt-Out tags
If your message contains an "opt-out" tag {OptOutURL}, the length and price returned will reflect the expansion of this tag into an actual, proper URL - which could potentially turn your message into a multi-part message, with more than one segment.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.
Short URLs
If your message contains URL's and you would like it to be replaced with a shorter URL, or you would like to use our system to track engagement with the link, you can provide this endpoint with an optional boolean parameter shorten_urls, when this value is true any well formed URL (i.e. Must start with http:// or https:// ) will be replaced with a shorter one.
If your account has a pURL (Personalised URL) configured, it will use that configured domain for the URL.
By default the shortened URLs will look like http://vc4.co/c/ABCD where the "ABCD" will be replaced with a unique code for your URL. Note, these URLs are 20 characters in length, if your URL is longer than 20 characters, it will end up longer than it was originally. Any pURL configurations may make this length longer as well.
Note: Each recipient in your batch will have their own unique URL code that can provide individualised engagement data.
Personalised Short URLs
The above short URL feature only covers the situation where a URL is in your message content. If desired, you can also use individual links for each of your recipients using the personalisation system. Instead of passing the boolean flag, to use short URLs, the personalisation field needs to be in a specific way.
Your message will need to contain the string {{ url | shorten_url }} and your to array will also required the url parameter set.
Required Parameters
You must include a to parameter. This value should be a list of recipient phone numbers in E.164 format.
You must also include a from parameter. This can be your phone number in E.164 format or an alphanumeric Sender ID.
Finally, you need to include a message parameter containing the message's content. If the number of characters in your message exceeds a certain length it is considered a "multi-part" message - and billed accordingly.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.
Basic usage example:
{
    "to" : ["+6141xxxxxx1", "+6141xxxxxx2"],
    "from": "Sender",
    "message": "Hi, this is an SMS message"
}


Personalised message example:
Data supplied for personalisation should be in the form of a list as above, the 'keys' of the to field will be the recipients mobile number, and the content of each item in the list will be the data used to personalise the message. (Data can be defined as both an array/list or as an object)
{
    "to" : {
        "+6141xxxxxx1": {"name": "Sample1"}, 
        "+6141xxxxxx2": {"name": "Sample2"},
        "+6141xxxxxx3": {},
    },
    "from": "Sender",
    "message": "Hi {{ name | default: 'there' }}, this is an SMS message"
}


This payload will result in 3 messages being sent with the following content:
Hi Sample1, this is an SMS message
Hi Sample2, this is an SMS message
Hi there, this is an SMS message

from.description: Your Sender ID - can be a longcode (required)

to.description: Array of mobile numbers to send to - limited to 1000 (required)

message.description: Your SMS message - the same message will be sent to all recipients (required)

shorten_urls.description: (Optional Field) - When true, any well formed URLs in your message will be replaced with shortened links

campaign_reference.description: (Optional Field) Used for custom report building

get.description:
Get list of recipients (contacts) against a specific SMS campaign. You can send a request and retrieve data about SMS recipients for a campaign at a time that suits you, using this endpoint - your Tall Bob SMS Campaign ID must be present in the URL path.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

Get a list of the templates your SMS Campaigns are built from - which is useful as the Tall Bob Template ID is required when sending an SMS Campaign. You can send a request and retrieve Campaign Templates at a time that suits you, using this endpoint.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

Get list of SMS campaigns for your account, by request. You can send a request and retrieve SMS campaigns at a time that suits you, using this endpoint.
Optional Filtering
You can filter your results by passing in optional query parameters to your request.

after - filter by setting lower limit on dates (timestamp)
before - filter by setting upper limit on dates (timestamp)

The format of timestamps in your request is reasonably flexible... For example, 01/01/1970, 2001-12-31, 2002-10-20T12:26:13+00:00, last Monday and yesterday are all examples of valid inputs for these types of request.
A Note about Postman Collections
If you have downloaded and imported this as a Postman Collection, you may find these optional query parameters have been selected with default values - which will probably not work.
If you are getting empty results back, check if these have been selected - and if they have, try again after deselecting them. You should then get back all your results, unfiltered.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

post.description:
You can send an SMS campaign using a campaign Template, allowing more advanced features such as Personalisation and Landing Pages, with Videos and even Forms.
Required Parameters
You must include a template_id parameter. This value should be a Tall Bob Campaign Template ID, which can be listed using another endpoint.
You must also include (only) one of the following parameters:

group_id - this is a Tall Bob Group ID - which represents a list of Tall Bob contacts - which can be as large a list as you need it to be
contact_ids - this is a list (array) of Tall Bob contacts, identified by their Tall Bob Contact ID - limited to 1000
external_ids - this is a list (array) of Tall Bob contacts, identified by your own Unique ID, which corresponds to the ID you use in your system to identify them - also limited to 1000

Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

post.summary: Send SMS Campaign To List of Mobiles
post.description:
You can send an SMS campaign to a list of mobile numbers. This allows more advanced features such as Personalisation and Landing Pages, Forms and Videos.
To support personalisation in both message content and Landing Pages, the list of mobile numbers that is sent to is created as 'Contacts' within our system.
Required Parameters
*   to - this is a list (array) of objects, these objects will be created as 'contacts' and then sent to. Array/list keys are mobiles, the data of each keyed object is any additional data to be stored with the contact. Note the maximum list size accepted is limited to 1000 contacts. To have more contacts in a campaign please see the information about the reference
*   from - This is your sender address, This can be your phone number in E.164 format or an alphanumeric Sender ID.
*   message - Your SMS message content to be sent. You are required to include a{{ campaign_link }} , this will be where your landing page will be linked to.
*   landing_page_id - The ID of your landing page, this is the page that will be linked to within your message.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.
Contact Data
Data provided in the to field will be saved/updated into individual contact records.
Contacts have a set of standard fields across all accounts - first_name, last_name, mobile, email, external_id .
Any additional fields sent will be stored as 'contact metadata'. If they do not exist, they will be created. These fields can then be used when building landing pages or messages from within the platform.
The external_id will be used as a primary key when deciding to create or update existing contacts. It is highly recommended to include an external_id from the requesting system. This external ID will be provided in any webhooks that have been configured so that the contact can be identified by your system.
To avoid excess duplicates being created within the platform, we will use the mobile number provided if this value is empty.
Personalisation:
Please see the information at this endpoint
Optional Parameters
*   video_id - The ID of the video that will be inserted on your landing page
*   reference - Your reference for the 'campaign'. This reference will be returned in any events connected via webhooks. The reference value also has a special use-case - Under the circumstances where multiple requests are required to send a complete campaign (when # of contacts is greater than 1000) providing the same reference will allows these multiple lists to be combined. When viewing this within the platform analytics, it will appear as a single campaign.

to.description: List of mobiles (and optional data) to sent campaign to

from.description: Your Sender ID - can be a longcode (required)

message.description: Your SMS message - the same message will be sent to all recipients (required)

reference.description: Your campaign reference - Using the same campaign reference will accross requests will combine multiple requests into a single campaign (optional)

get.description:
Retrieve details about a specific SMS campaign, by Campaign ID using this endpoint - your Tall Bob SMS Campaign ID must be present in the URL path.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

Retrieve details about a specific Campaign Template by ID, using this endpoint - your Tall Bob Campaign Template ID must be present in the URL path.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

post.description:
This endpoint has been made available to assist customers while exploring and developing integrations.
This endpoint is only available on our test systems (api-test.tallbob.com)
Use of this endpoint requires a longcode to have been assigned to your test account. To have one of these assigned, please contact support@tallbob.com to have this arranged.
Sending a request to this endpoint will 'fake' an inbound message to the 'longcode' in the request, from 'sender'. 
This message will be processed in the same way a real message would get processed on our production system. This includes activating any triggers and generating a 'message_received' payload when a web hook has been setup.
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
A successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

longcode.description: A longcode assigned to your account (A mobile number in E.164 format). This is the number being sent to. (required)

sender.description: Single mobile number. This will be the number that the message is being sent from (required)

post.description:
This endpoint  provides clients a mobile lookup to complete number validation in real time.
Required Parameters
Every request must contain:

a mobile value, representing the Mobile number

 
Note: The mobile field must conform to the E.164 format

Post data to create a new webhook with details provided. Upon successful creation, details of your new webhook will be returned - including an ID, which is required by other methods to identify your newly created webhook.
Required Parameters
Every request must contain a url value, representing the URL to post data to, and a value for the event_type which represents the trigger for sending the data to your webhook. Neither parameter can contain an empty value. The url MUST be an SSL-encrypted endpoint (beginning with https://) with a valid certificate.
Event Types
Valid event types are as follows:

message_polling - Message prepared for delivery
message_pushed - Message sent
message_delivered - Message delivered
message_delivery_error - Delivery failed
message_received - Response received
message_received_mms - MMS Response received
message_opened - Email opened
impression - Landing Page viewed
clicktrack - Contact clicked ordinary link
unsubscribe_track - Contact clicked unsubscribe link
unsubscribe - Contact opted out
video_play - Video played
video_complete - Video ended
form_submission - Form submitted
scratchie_initiated - Scratchie rendered
scratchie_interact - Scratchie interacted with

Optional Parameters
If your webhook requires additional information to be included in the header, such as a Basic Auth string, these can also be included in your request. Currently only two such optional parameters are supported:
authorization
You can set the authorisation header used when posting to your webhooks using this parameter. It should be the entire auth string.
eg Basic <>
content-type
You can set the "Content-Type" header used when posting to your webhooks using this parameter. By default, this is already set to application-json so if your webhook supports posting JSON data, you do not need to set this value.
Note: only two types are currently supported - application-json and application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

event_type.description: Event type (see above for valid event types)

enabled.description: Whether or not the webhook should be enabled (optional)

authorization.description: Auth header if required to POST to your webhook (optional)

content-type.description: Content Type header if default JSON type unsupported (optional)

get.description:
List of webhooks for your account, by request. You can send a request and retrieve your list at a time that suits you, using this endpoint.
Optional filtering
You can also filter results by url and/or enabled status, by passing these in as (optional) query parameters.
A Note about Postman Collections
If you have downloaded and imported this as a Postman Collection, you may find these optional query parameters have been selected with default values - which will probably not work.
If you are getting empty results back, check if these have been selected - and if they have, try again after deselecting them. You should then get back all your results, unfiltered.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

delete.description:
Delete a specific webhook using this endpoint - your webhook ID must be present in the URL path.
Success Response
Note: this endpoint does not return any content upon success.
Furthermore, a successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of 204 instead of the usual 200

put.description:
You can update an existing webhook using this endpoint - your webhook ID must be present in the URL path.
Similar rules to the Create method apply, except there are no required parameters.
Header Parameters
You can update existing header configurations for your webhook and you can also remove a header by setting the value to null.

event_type.description: Event type (see above for valid event types)

enabled.description: Boolean value to enable or disable webhook

authorization.description: Auth header if required to POST to your webhook

content-type.description: Content Type header if default JSON type unsupported

get.description:
Retrieve details about a specific webhook using this endpoint - your webhook ID must be present in the URL path.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

post.description:
Post data to create a new account with details provided. Upon successful creation, details of your new account will be returned - including the Tall Bob Api auth_token, which is used for authentication.
Required Parameters
Every request must contain a account_name value, representing the account's name. Parameter cannot contain an empty value.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.
Success Response
A successful response will have an HTTP STATUS of usual 200  and will return a newly created account object, along with its own auth_token

account_name.description: Name of the account to be created

get.description:
Get the current balance of your account using this endpoint.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

List of accounts created via Api for your account, by request. You can send a request and retrieve your list at a time that suits you, using this endpoint.
Optional filtering
You can also filter results by enabled status, by passing these in as (optional) query parameter.
Please refer to HTTP STATUS CODES for definitions of response status codes for this request.

put.description: You can update an existing account using this endpoint - the account ID must be present in the URL path.

basicAuth.description: Use your API username and API key from the Tall Bob platform
